statement: Window execution should support pause_after at least for the collect stage (pause_after='collect') before running L1-L3 transforms, because it provides a high-leverage gate for pilot debugging and makes it possible to validate evidence quality and scrape yield before paying transformation costs.
rationale: The current WindowWorkflow already supports pauseAfter in its workflow input, but Convex does not expose it. Adding a collect gate provides a simple 'stop after evidence ingest' checkpoint, which matches the run rubric-gate pattern and reduces wasted LLM spend when scrapes are empty, wrong-country, or dominated by boilerplate.
keyAssumptions: The window pipeline will remain a single workflow for the next pilot iteration, or adding pause_after will not conflict with later decomposition work
Users/agents will actively inspect or validate evidence yield between collect and transforms during pilot hardening
validationCriteria: A window started with pause_after='collect' reliably pauses after evidence insertion and before any transform attempts are written
Resume continues transforms without duplicating collect inserts
critiquePoints: If the window pipeline is immediately decomposed into raw-collection and transform workflows, adding pause_after to the monolithic workflow may be short-lived work
questions: Should pause_after='collect' be enforced by workflow logic only, or mirrored into the windows domain row to match run semantics?
